title: alphitonia incana
intro: Alphitonia incana, also known as the Jarrah or Queensland Jarrah, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of northern Australia and New Guinea, belonging to the family Rhamnaceae. The plant's leaves and bark are used in traditional medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Alphitonia incana is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and it is also used as a treatment for wounds and ulcers. Herbal preparations of the plant include decoctions and infusions, which are made from the leaves, bark, and roots, and are used to treat a range of health problems, including respiratory and digestive issues.
